rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned expressions.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers exist in the current scope and are properly typed.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in AGREE contracts to prevent resolution errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match the expected syntax (int, real, bool) to avoid reference resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes.
Assignments in AGREE should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
Do not reference undefined or mismatched types in AGREE expressions without proper declaration.
